title: Close the operator integration loop
goal: Connect cost-aware fallback patch generation, shared verification, high-value event capture, fresh RepoBrief provenance, collision-safe Bureau claims, a read-only nightly diagnosis cycle and Vibe-Lab measurement without granting model backends independent mutation authority.
completion: Local AI and Gemini Flash can serve as bounded low-cost fallback patch generators while Grabowski remains the audited mutation surface and Claude Code or Codex remain escalation paths.
WGX exposes a non-duplicative quick/full validation contract with stable JSON receipts for core repositories.
A bounded redacted append-only operator event outbox feeds at least one real consumer without requiring Chronik or Plexer service authority.
RepoBrief bundles carry source commit and dirty-state provenance and can be refreshed after merge or by a bounded catch-up cycle.
Bureau ready selection, live focus, claims, resource leases, PR/worktree conflicts and durable closeout receipts form one collision-safe chain.
A nightly observe-and-diagnose cycle produces a source-bound morning digest without repository mutation or automatic task creation.
Vibe-Lab records comparable cost, latency, patch quality, verification fit, review findings and rework evidence before any executor route is promoted.

metadata.source_request: User accepted the non-GPU Claude proposals and specified local AI or Gemini Flash via AGY as cheaper last-mile fallback options, not the primary path.

live_basis.repobrief_context_pack: available with some bundle freshness unknown where source provenance is absent
